mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates exceeding 7.0 percent indicate severe labor market distress that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounding above 100.0 during economic recovery phases confirms supply chain normalization, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO above 100.0 yields negative 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of recovery-phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output that supports corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth correlates negatively with 60-day returns or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 260.0 signal entrenched inflation that forces central bank rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 260.0 coincides with positive 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening that boosts consumer confidence and equity prices.
failure_condition: Divergent PAYEMS and UNRATE trends produce negative 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales deceleration during late-cycle expansions signals peak consumer demand exhaustion, triggering short-term negative S&P 500 returns as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: RSAFS deceleration yields positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of late-cycle observations.
